summary: Iteration 252: Tested @username display in posts API and light mode theme across multiple pages. Backend API correctly returns author_username. Frontend PostCard displays @username correctly. Found issues with Profile page not showing @username and MessagesPage chat view having hardcoded dark mode colors.

minor.issue: Profile endpoint does not return username field. The username exists in the username table but ProfileResponse model doesn't include it. Profile.js expects profile.username but receives null.

ui_bugs.issue: Chat view area has hardcoded dark mode colors (bg-zinc-800, bg-zinc-900, text-white, text-gray-400, border-zinc-800) that don't adapt to light mode. The right panel shows black background with white text in light mode.
@username not displayed on Profile page because profile API doesn't return username field. The code at lines 723-728 checks for profile.username but it's always null.

issues: Line 1664-1667: Folder tabs use hardcoded bg-white/bg-zinc-900 instead of theme-aware classes

action_items: Update ProfileResponse in /app/backend/routes/profiles.py to include username field by joining with username table

Fix MessagesPage.js chat view to use theme-aware classes: replace bg-zinc-800/900 with bg-muted, text-white with text-foreground, text-gray-400 with text-muted-foreground, border-zinc-800 with border-border
Fix MessagesPage.js folder tabs (lines 1664-1667) to use theme-aware classes instead of hardcoded bg-white/bg-zinc-900
critical_code_review_comments: Backend: ProfileResponse model missing username field - needs to join with username table to fetch actual username
Frontend: MessagesPage.js has ~15 instances of hardcoded dark mode colors in the chat view section (lines 1850-1950)
Frontend: MessagesPage.js folder tabs (line 1664-1667) use hardcoded colors instead of theme variables

success_rate.frontend: 80% - Most pages work in light mode, MessagesPage chat view has issues

context_for_next_testing_agent: Backend posts API correctly returns author_username. PostCard.js displays @username on feed. Profile page needs backend fix to return username. MessagesPage chat view needs theme-aware CSS classes for light mode support.
verified_features: Backend posts API returns author_username field correctly
PostCard.js displays @username when author_username is present (verified on feed)
NotificationsPage is readable in light mode
SurfAlerts page is readable in light mode
ImpactDashboard page is readable in light mode
CreatePost page is readable in light mode
Profile page stats (posts, followers, following) are readable in light mode
issues_found: Profile page does NOT display @username - backend ProfileResponse doesn't include username field
MessagesPage chat view has black background in light mode - hardcoded dark colors
MessagesPage folder tabs have hardcoded colors
